title: huperzia henryi
intro: Huperzia henryi is a species of liverwort, a type of non-vascular plant that belongs to the moss family. It is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme involved in the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter associated with memory and cognitive function. The plant is rich in huperzine A, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, and has been used to treat conditions such as Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, and stroke. Huperzia henryi is often prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or extracts, which are used to support brain health, improve cognitive function, and enhance memory.
